name: the-science-of-leonardo-inside-the-mind-of-the-great-genius-of-the-renaissance
description: Fritjof Capra's revelatory look at Leonardo da Vinci as a scientist — his observation-based method, study of patterns, water, flight, anatomy, and a systems view of nature that foreshadows modern ecology.
useCase: Understand Leonardo da Vinci's scientific method — observation, pattern recognition, and systems thinking — and how it differs from modern mechanistic science.
knownRisks: Capra's interpretation differs from mainstream Leonardo scholarship
Some may find the systems-thinking emphasis too modernizing
Assumes interest in both art and science
